perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, except, in each case, to the extent that Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including any filings required (and disclosures contained therein) under Section 13(d) of the Exchange Act) or by legal process (including by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, or similar process) to disclose any such information; provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, without exceptions. provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 3 allowed for disclosure under legal obligations. The modified version removes the 'except' clause, mandating strict confidentiality 'without exceptions', but it goes on saying 'provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight'. This creates a contradiction because it first states there are no exceptions to confidentiality and later describes a specific situation in which the Receiving Party may disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall (a) keep and maintain any Material Blueknight Non-public Information in strict confidence and (b) not disclose to any other person the fact that Blueknight Non-public Information has been made available to Receiving Party, without exceptions. provided, however, that Receiving Party may disclose such information to such of its representatives who need such information for the purpose of evaluating or assisting with Receiving Party’s investment in Blueknight, it being understood that such representatives shall be informed in advance by Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the provisions of this letter by such representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exceptions for legally required disclosures from the original text. It then states 'without exceptions'. However, it then proceeds to say that the Receiving Party may disclose the information to its representatives. This creates a direct contradiction, as the text first states there are no exceptions, and immediately after, it provides an exception. This contradicts the earlier statement that there are no exceptions, causing an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation and adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: “Blueknight Non-public Information” shall mean information pertaining to Blueknight, the General Partner and/or their respective Affiliates that has not been Publicly Disclosed and that Blueknight or the General Partner discloses to Receiving Party, including, without limitation, the existence of the Refinancing Meeting (as defined below) and attendance by Receiving Party or any other limited partner of Blueknight at such meeting and all information regarding the refinancing disclosed, discussed or made available to Receiving Party at the Refinancing Meeting. The term Blueknight Non-public Information shall not include, however, information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was or becomes available to Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior to or after its disclosure by Blueknight or the General Partner from a person, other than Blueknight or the General Partner, who is not known by Receiving Party to be bound by an obligation to Blueknight or the General Partner to keep such information confidential or (iii) is developed independently by Receiving Party without use of Blueknight Non-public Information and without violating any of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: “Blueknight Non-public Information” shall mean all information pertaining to Blueknight, the General Partner and/or their respective Affiliates disclosed to Receiving Party, without exception. The term Blueknight Non-public Information shall not include, however, information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was or becomes available to Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior to or after its disclosure by Blueknight or the General Partner from a person, other than Blueknight or the General Partner, who is not known by Receiving Party to be bound by an obligation to Blueknight or the General Partner to keep such information confidential or (iii) is developed independently by Receiving Party without use of Blueknight Non-public Information and without violating any of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Blueknight Non-public Information" specifies it is information 'that has not been Publicly Disclosed'. The modified version defines it as 'all information pertaining to Blueknight…disclosed to Receiving Party, without exception'. This creates a contradiction because it suggests *all* information shared is confidential, even information already in the public domain, which contradicts the exclusions listed later in the definition itself. The inclusion of 'without exception' creates the conflict with the exceptions detailed within the rest of the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions . (a) “Affiliate” has the meaning provided in Rule 12b-2 of the Exchange Act. (b) “Blueknight Non-public Information” shall mean all information pertaining to Blueknight, the General Partner and/or their respective Affiliates disclosed to Receiving Party, without exception. The term Blueknight Non-public Information shall not include, however, information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was or becomes available to Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis prior to or after its disclosure by Blueknight or the General Partner from a person, other than Blueknight or the General Partner, who is not known by Receiving Party to be bound by an obligation to Blueknight or the General Partner to keep such information confidential or (iii) is developed independently by Receiving Party without use of Blueknight Non-public Information and without violating any of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines "Blueknight Non-public Information" as 'all information pertaining to Blueknight…disclosed to Receiving Party, without exception.' This directly contradicts the later part of the same definition in the contradicted text, which explicitly excludes certain types of information (e.g., publicly available information) from being considered Blueknight Non-public Information. The 'without exception' clause creates an in-text contradiction with the stated exceptions.

perturbation.original_text: Blueknight and the General Partner agree that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, unless specifically consented to in writing by Receiving Party, Blueknight will not file a proxy statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission relating to the Unitholder Meeting (as defined in the Global Transaction Agreement, dated as of October 25, 2010, by and among the General Partner, Blueknight and the purchasers set forth in Schedule I thereto).
perturbation.changed_text: Blueknight and the General Partner agree that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, Blueknight will file a proxy statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission relating to the Unitholder Meeting (as defined in the Global Transaction Agreement, dated as of October 25, 2010, by and among the General Partner, Blueknight and the purchasers set forth in Schedule I thereto).
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 4(c) states Blueknight will *not* file a proxy statement for 20 days, *unless* consented to by the Receiving Party. The modified text mandates that Blueknight *will* file the proxy statement, removing the conditional clause, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Blueknight and the General Partner agree that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, Blueknight will file a proxy statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission relating to the Unitholder Meeting (as defined in the Global Transaction Agreement, dated as of October 25, 2010, by and among the General Partner, Blueknight and the purchasers set forth in Schedule I thereto).
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Blueknight would *not* file a proxy statement for 20 days unless consented to, while the changed text mandates that Blueknight *will* file the proxy statement, removing the conditional clause. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that Blueknight *will* file a proxy statement. The structural flaw of placing contradictory obligations within the contract makes the obligations unclear and creates an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, unless specifically consented to in writing by Blueknight, Receiving Party and its controlled Affiliates will not, directly or indirectly, effect any sale (including any short-sale) or acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or acquisition of assets of Blueknight or any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, Receiving Party and its controlled Affiliates will, directly or indirectly, effect any sale (including any short-sale) or acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or acquisition of assets of Blueknight or any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Receiving Party will *not* effect any sale or acquisition for 20 days unless with Blueknight's consent. The modified text removes the 'unless specifically consented to in writing by Blueknight' clause and now stipulates the Receiving Party *will* effect sales or acquisitions, which contradicts the initial intent to restrict those activities. This removal changes the core commitment of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party agrees that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, Receiving Party and its controlled Affiliates will, directly or indirectly, effect any sale (including any short-sale) or acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or acquisition of assets of Blueknight or any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the Receiving Party from effecting sales or acquisitions for 20 days unless Blueknight consents. The changed text removes the consent clause, now stating the Receiving Party *will* effect such sales or acquisitions during that period. This directly contradicts the implied restriction in the original agreement and creates a conflict with the Contradicted Text which is an exact replica of the Changed text, therefore, meaning there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that none of Blueknight, the General Partner nor any of their respective representatives makes any representations or warranties, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any Blueknight Non-public Information, that none of Blueknight, the General Partner nor any of their respective representatives shall have any liability whatsoever to Receiving Party or any other person as a result of their use of any Blueknight Non-public Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom and that Receiving Party shall assume full and exclusive responsibility for any conclusions derived from the Blueknight Non-public Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that Blueknight, the General Partner and their respective representatives make representations or warranties, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any Blueknight Non-public Information, that Blueknight, the General Partner and their respective representatives shall have liability whatsoever to Receiving Party or any other person as a result of their use of any Blueknight Non-public Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom and that Receiving Party shall assume full and exclusive responsibility for any conclusions derived from the Blueknight Non-public Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original version explicitly states that Blueknight makes no warranties about the information's accuracy and has no liability for its use. The modification removes the word `none` from the first and second instances, directly negating the original statement. This creates a direct contradiction because the text first denies and then affirms warranties and liabilities related to the Blueknight Non-public Information. The insertion will make the Receiving Party think the are responsible for any conclusion derived.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that Blueknight, the General Partner and their respective representatives make representations or warranties, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any Blueknight Non-public Information, that Blueknight, the General Partner and their respective representatives shall have liability whatsoever to Receiving Party or any other person as a result of their use of any Blueknight Non-public Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom and that Receiving Party shall assume full and exclusive responsibility for any conclusions derived from the Blueknight Non-public Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the word 'none' from the original statement, thereby affirming warranties and liabilities related to Blueknight Non-public Information, which contradicts the original intention of disclaiming any such warranties or liabilities. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. This confirms a strong contradiction exists because the Receiving Party would now assume they are responsible, conflicting with prior lack of warranty.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the Receiving Party can not transfer or assign the agreement without prior written consent of Blueknight. The modified text removes the `not` and makes the agreement transferable/assignable without Blueknight's consent creating a contradiction. Removing `not` changes the core of this clause and creates contradictory terms about assignment rights in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be transferred or assigned (by operation of law or otherwise) by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of Blueknight, and shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party may *not* transfer or assign the agreement without Blueknight's consent. The changed text removes "not", now permitting the Receiving Party to transfer or assign the agreement without consent. The contradicted text is identical to the original text, meaning the changed text directly contradicts it regarding the Receiving Party's ability to transfer or assign the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction. This is a structural flaw contradiction because the same clause exists in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
